title: Poems From "A Shropshire Lad" - LX
content:
Now hollow fires burn out to black,
    And lights are guttering low:
    Square your shoulders, lift your pack,
    And leave your friends and go.

    Oh never fear, man, nought's to dread,
    Look not left nor right:
    In all the endless road you tread
    There's nothing but the night.
meaning:
Alfred Edward Housman's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Poems From "A Shropshire Lad" - LX"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
